title: 1. Input data and required pre-processing
text: Understanding genetic resistance therapy emerges central problem cancer genomics. increasing adoption high-throughput sequencing clinical practice, often possible identify driver event responsible disease relapse. However, even longitudinal data available, resolution frequently low determine whether resistant clone already present treatment onset, subsequently selected, whether emerged treatment began. Resolving question direct clinical implications: patients harbouring pre-existing resistant clone may benefit given therapy, since many treatments mutagenic, also important identify cases treatment may accelerated emergence resistance. TOSCA R package leverages longitudinal whole-genome sequencing (WGS) data combined clinical information infer, real time, timing origin clonal expansions associated therapy resistance. TOSCA employs Bayesian model mutation accumulation described Poisson process, infers timing events associated changes process intensity, along tumour dynamic parameters growth rate mutation rate therapy. required inputs : Clinical information: dates longitudinal sample collection dates therapy administration (therapies associated known mutational signatures). Mutation information: number mutations private resistant clone, stratified mutational signature karyotype. vignette, walk complete analysis longitudinal WGS data single patient acute myeloid leukaemia (AML) developed resistance immunotherapy. example drawn TOSCA reference paper illustrates package can used identify interpret clinically relevant genomic events real-world setting.

title: 1. Input data and required pre-processing
text: UPN06 drawn cohort 11 AML patients developed resistance allogeneic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT) copy-neutral loss heterozygosity (CNLOH) HLA genes located short arm (p-arm) chromosome 6. begin loading data required inference:
code: library(TOSCA) #> Loading required package: ggplot2 #> Loading required package: dplyr #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union data("UPN06")

title: 1. Input data and required pre-processing
text: first input records dates sample collection YYYY-mm-dd format. date birth optional.
code: UPN06$Input$Samples #>             Name       Date #> 1          Birth 1993-01-01 #> 2 Pre-Transplant 2014-03-21 #> 3        Relapse 2016-08-11

title: 1. Input data and required pre-processing
text: second input records therapies either associated known mutational signature can induce dormant state cancer cells — instance, cytarabine AML. Start end dates must follow YYYY-mm-dd format sample dates. therapy must classified one : Mutagenic: associated known SBS signature. Chemotherapy inducing dormancy: capable inducing dormant state cancer cells (e.g. cytarabine AML).
code: UPN06$Input$Therapies #>          Name                          Class      Start        End #> 1  Cytarabine Chemotherapy inducing dormancy 2012-02-01 2013-12-01 #> 2 Ganciclovir                      Mutagenic 2014-05-19 2014-06-25

title: 1. Input data and required pre-processing
text: third input contains mutations located phylogenetic branch associated relapse. Constructing input requires two upstream analyses completed: Clonal deconvolution reconstruct bulk phylogeny assign mutations branches (e.g. MOBSTER). Signature deconvolution attribute mutations branch SBS signatures (e.g. SigProfiler, SparseSignatures, BASCULE). TOSCA requires private mutations longitudinal sample, stratified signature. Specifically: Clock-like primary: diploid private mutations first sample attributed clock-like signatures (SBS1 SBS5). Clock-like relapse: diploid private mutations second sample attributed clock-like signatures. Treatment-associated relapse: diploid private mutations second sample attributed therapy-associated signature (named drug). Alpha: mutations present double copy CNA. Beta: mutations present single copy CNA.
code: UPN06$Input$Mutations #>              Name     Length Karyotype               Type Value #> 1 m_clock_primary 2780524307       1:1 clock-like primary     0 #> 2         m_clock 2780524307       1:1 clock-like relapse   841 #> 3           m_cyt 2780524307       1:1         Cytarabine     0 #> 4           m_gcv 2780524307       1:1        Ganciclovir  1347 #> 5           alpha   47911693       2:0              alpha    20 #> 6            beta   47911693       2:0               beta     1

title: 1. Input data and required pre-processing

TOSCA flags mutation type name match reserved keywords (clock-like primary, clock-like relapse, alpha, beta, driver) guard accidental mismatches Mutations Therapies dataframes. , Cytarabine Ganciclovir intentional: correspond therapy-associated mutational processes names match exactly entries UPN06$Input$Therapies, required inference proceed correctly. inference results slot empty model fit, shown next section.
code: x <- TOSCA::init(   samples    = UPN06$Input$Samples,   therapies  = UPN06$Input$Therapies,   mutations  = UPN06$Input$Mutations,   parameters = UPN06$Input$Parameters,   sample_name = "UPN06" ) #> Warning in TOSCA:::check_genomic_input(mutations): Non-standard mutation #> type(s): Cytarabine , Ganciclovir. If therapy-associated, ensure the name #> matches the corresponding entry in the Therapies dataframe. Valid reserved #> types: clock-like primary , clock-like relapse , alpha , beta , driver x #> ──  #>  #> ** TOSCA ** Sample: UPN06  ──────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Clinical History (~ 2 years, 4 months and 24 days) #> ── Pre-Transplant (2014-03-21) --> Cytarabine (669 days) --> Ganciclovir (37 day #>  #> ── Timed events:  1 CNAs with dormancy without Drivers altering the mutation rate #>  #> ── Inference results #> [1] "Fit not available"

title: 2. Setting the priors
text: TOSCA models mutation accumulation inhomogeneous Poisson process whose rate varies time according clinical history patient latent timing clonal events. Mutations partitioned groups mutational process karyotype, group accumulates independently. group ss, expected mutation count : ms∼Poisson(ns⋅ℓs⋅ω⋅∫tstarttendμsdt)m_s \sim \text{Poisson}\left( n_s \cdot \ell_s \cdot \omega \cdot \int_{t_{\text{start}}}^{t_{\text{end}}} \mu_s \, dt \right) nsn_s number alleles (e.g. 2 diploid mutations), ℓs\ell_s length genomic region mutations accumulate, μs\mu_s mutation rate group ss, ω\omega tumour growth rate. Parameter inference performed via Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC). TOSCA infers two sets latent quantities: Timing parameters: time origin early common ancestor (ECA), time origin resistant clone, time origin recent common ancestor (MRCA). Dynamic parameters: tumour growth rate ω\omega mutation rate(s) μs\mu_s therapy.

title: 2. Setting the priors
text: hyperparameters required run TOSCA fall three groups.

title: 2. Setting the priors
text: priors ECA, MRCA, resistant clone origin times set automatically uniform distributions, bounds derived clinical history provided initialisation. Ordering constraints events enforced construction (e.g. ECA must precede MRCA), clinical information restricts admissible intervals: instance, therapy-associated mutational process detected, TOSCA assumes MRCA originated earlier start corresponding treatment. user input required priors.

title: 2. Setting the priors
text: mutation rate μclock\mu_{\text{clock}} governs mutation accumulation clock-like processes (SBS1 SBS5) treated known constant. can estimated running MOBSTER univariate VAF spectrum primary sample. recommend using primary sample estimate, presence additional mutational processes active relapse can confound inference. samples show non-clock-like signature activity, use sample appears monoclonal scale MOBSTER estimate SBS1+SBS5 exposure tail mutations. UPN06:
code: library(TOSCA) data("UPN06")  UPN06$Input$Parameters %>% filter(Name == "mu_clock") #>       Name        Value Index #> 1 mu_clock 4.706432e-08  <NA>

title: 2. Setting the priors
text: growth rate ω\omega therapy-associated mutation rate(s) μs\mu_s assigned Gamma prior parametrised shape (α\alpha) rate (β\beta). encode prior beliefs plausible range tumour growth mutagenicity treatment. UPN06:
code: UPN06$Input$Parameters %>%    filter(Name %in% c("alpha_th_step", "beta_th_step", "omega_alpha", "omega_beta")) #>            Name     Value       Index #> 1 alpha_th_step        40 Ganciclovir #> 2  beta_th_step 190476190 Ganciclovir #> 3   omega_alpha        10        <NA> #> 4    omega_beta         1        <NA>

title: 2. Setting the priors
text: minimum maximum number cells relapse lineage primary sampling relapse constrain support timing inference. defaults (10610^6 101310^{13}) can adjusted clinical biological information suggests otherwise. Users can also specify whether apply likelihood regularisation term assuming exponential growth first /second sample, via exponential_growth parameter, takes value 1 (enabled) 0 (disabled) sample independently. UPN06:
code: UPN06$Input$Parameters %>%    filter(Name %in% c("exponential_growth", "N_min", "N_max")) #>                 Name Value          Index #> 1 exponential_growth 0e+00 Pre-Transplant #> 2 exponential_growth 1e+00        Relapse #> 3              N_min 1e+06 Pre-Transplant #> 4              N_max 1e+13 Pre-Transplant #> 5              N_min 1e+08        Relapse #> 6              N_max 1e+11        Relapse

title: 2. Setting the priors
text: Two additional parameters allow finer control model behaviour. k_step parameter controls steepness function regulating onset decay therapy-associated mutagenesis, , rapidly mutational process activates treatment begins fades ends. phi parameters introduce overdispersion term around expected mutation counts group, accommodating uncertainty observed mutation tallies. UPN06:
code: UPN06$Input$Parameters %>%    filter(Name %in% c("k_step", "phi_clock", "phi_th_step", "phi_cna_alpha", "phi_cna_beta")) #>            Name   Value       Index #> 1        k_step 2.5e+03        <NA> #> 2     phi_clock 1.0e-03        <NA> #> 3   phi_th_step 1.0e-03 Ganciclovir #> 4 phi_cna_alpha 1.0e-03    47911693 #> 5  phi_cna_beta 1.0e-03    47911693

title: 3. Running the inference
text: inputs place TOSCA object correctly initialised, inference launched single call fit(). function takes TOSCA object input populates inference results slot. resistances mediated CNA event, case UPN06, model_name set "CNA". dormancy argument set TRUE patient received chemotherapeutic agent known induce tumour cell dormancy, cytarabine. Additional arguments allow fine-grained control MCMC sampler (number chains, iterations per chain, warmup length, ); see ?TOSCA::fit full details. Upon completion, fit() prints summary report containing: Convergence diagnostics: whether R̂<1.01\hat{R} < 1.01, number divergent transitions, whether E-BFMI fell 0.3 chain. Preliminary posterior predictive check: whether observed mutation count group falls mode 90th percentile posterior predictive distribution. Parameter summary: posterior estimates inferred parameter, including posterior predictives. summary inference results can retrieved time : fitted object also exposes raw posterior draws, model specification, diagnostic summary directly:

title: 4. Visualising the results
text: inferred timing posteriors can visualised patient’s clinical timeline two complementary ways: histograms overlaid single timeline, stacked densities. plots, treatment periods shown coloured rectangles sampling dates points timeline.   default x-axis expressed calendar dates. preferred, timings can displayed days relative clinically meaningful reference event. UPN06, use allo-HSCT date origin:  Summary statistics can likewise computed relative reference date using days_from(). particularly useful classifying timed event de novo (originating reference) pre-existing (originating ). CNA-mediated resistance, parameter t_cna length corresponding CNA, specified mutations input, must provided: Consistent findings reported reference paper, posterior places CNLOH HLA locus allo-HSCT close temporal proximity ganciclovir exposure, supporting de novo origin resistance event.
code: TOSCA::plot_timing(UPN06) TOSCA::plot_timing_MAP(UPN06) allo_HSCT <- "2014-04-23" TOSCA::plot_timing_days(UPN06, days_from = allo_HSCT, event_name = "allo-HSCT") TOSCA::days_from(UPN06, from = allo_HSCT, parameter = "t_cna", cna_length = 47911693) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   median  mean    q5      q95      #>   <drtn>  <drtn>  <drtn>  <drtn>   #> 1 95 days 96 days 65 days 132 days

title: 4. Visualising the results
text: assess inferred dynamic parameters, plot_prior_vs_posterior() displays prior density alongside posterior histogram parameter. posterior median indicated vertical red line.

title: 4. Visualising the results
text: verify inferred parameters compatible observed data, plot_ppc() displays multivariate posterior predictive distribution (PPD) mutation group. observed mutation count shown red point; PPD represented cloud draws, colour-coded distance observed value.

title: 4. Visualising the results
text: TOSCA exposes suite diagnostic plots built bayesplot package. inspected routinely verify sampler explored posterior adequately. Available diagnostics include chain trace plots, R̂\hat{R} plots, effective sample size (ESS) plots, divergent transitions.

what: TOSCA - Timing Of Somatic Copy number Alterations
title: TOSCA - Timing Of Somatic Copy number Alterations
text: TOSCA (Timing Somatic Events Cancer), framework leverages longitudinal whole genome sequencing data time clonal expantions associated drug resistance. uses Bayesian Poisson process model mutation accumulation along phylogenetic branch connecting Early Common Ancestor (ECA) Recent Common Ancestor (MRCA), genetic resistance emerges. framework infers timing resistance-associated genetic events alter intensity mutation accumulation process, copy-number alterations point mutations associated hypermutator phenotype. Importantly, inferred timings mapped onto patient’s clinical history, enabling events interpreted real time rather solely pseudotime.

title: TOSCA - Timing Of Somatic Copy number Alterations
text: use TOSCA, please cite: * Timing origin genetic therapy resistance human cancers Riccardo Bergamin, Alice Antonello, Giulio Caravagna.

title: TOSCA - Timing Of Somatic Copy number Alterations
text: can install released version GitHub : Note: TOSCA requires cmdstanr fit models. Please follow installation instructions use.

title: Infer the timing of the events of interest fitting the appropriate TOSCA model — fit
code: fit(   x,   n_iterations = 10000,   n_chains = 4,   warm_up = 5000,   cores = 4,   adapt_delta = 0.95,   stepsize = 0.01,   seed = 5,   model_name = "Driver",   dormancy = F,   verbose = F,   initialisation = F,   init_fun = NULL,   max_mrca = NA,   reg_dormancy = 0 )

title: Infer the timing of the events of interest fitting the appropriate TOSCA model — fit
text: x TOSCA object n_iterations Number iteration MCMC chain. default 10000. n_chains positive integer specifying number Markov chains. default 4. warm_up positive integer specifying number warmup (aka burnin) iterations per chain. default 5000. cores Number cores use executing chains parallel adapt_delta target acceptance probability Metropolis step HMC. default 0.95. stepsize far particle moves leapfrog step along trajectory. default 0.01. seed seed computation. Default 5. model_name Name model: "CNA" "Driver" dormancy Boolean specifying model include dormancy (available "CNA" model). default F verbose Boolen specifying fit function output iterations progression. default F. initialisation Boolean, default= F. true, must specify init_fun init_fun Vector initial values used initialisation = T. max_mrca maximun date t_mrca reg_dormancy Boolean (0 1). reg_dormancy = 0, likelihood regularisation imposed timing dormancy end

title: Infer the timing of the events of interest fitting the appropriate TOSCA model — fit
text: TOSCA object

title: Infer the timing of the events of interest fitting the appropriate TOSCA model — fit
code: library(TOSCA) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) data("exampleData_CNA") set.seed(123) mutations = exampleData_CNA$Mutations parameters = exampleData_CNA$Parameters samples = exampleData_CNA$Samples therapies = exampleData_CNA$Therapies x = init(mutations=mutations, samples=samples, therapies=therapies, parameters=parameters) #> Error in init(mutations = mutations, samples = samples, therapies = therapies,     parameters = parameters): Input must be a data.frame. fit = TOSCA::fit(x, model_name='CNA',                  n_iterations = 10000,                  n_chains = 4,                  warm_up = 5000) #> Error: object 'x' not found

title: Initialise TOSCA object — init
text: mutations data.frame information mutation falling timed branches. 1. Name (str), name choosing cluster mutations 2. Length (int), length genomic segment sit 3. Karyptype (str), karyotype genomic segment sit "Major:minor" (ex "1:1") 4. Type (str), one following: "clock-like primary", "clock-like relapse", "alpha", "beta", "driver", name mutagenic drug associated reported therapies dataframe 5. Value (int), number mutations parameters data.frame parameters required inference. 1. Name (str), 2. Value (real), 3. Index (int) samples data.frame name collection date longitudinal samples + date birth (optional). 1. Name (str), name sample 2. Date (date) date collection format "YYYY-mm-dd" therapies data.frame name exposure period therapies affecting mutation accumulation process. 1. Name (str) 2. Class (str), one following : "Mutagenic step", "Mutagenic cauchy", "Driver responsive", "Chemotherapy inducing dormancy" 3. Start (date), start cicle therapy format "YYYY-mm-dd" 4. End (date), end cicle therapy format "YYYY-mm-dd" sample_name string name sample
